LAW AND MOTION 301, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT WEIL-MCLAIN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY PRESENTS DISPUTE OVER A MATERIAL FACT, NAMELY, WHETHER OR NOT HE WORKED ON DEFENDANT'S BOILERS AND THEY WERE "FALLING APART". SO DOES HIS RESPONSE TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NUMBER 1 IN WHICH HE FLATLY SO STATES: DEFENDANT FAILS TO INCLUDE A COPY OF THOSE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND THUS CANNOT SHOW IT SERVED INTERROGATORIES REQUESTING ALL FACTS SUPPORTING PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM OF ASBESTOS EXPOSURE. DEFENDANT FAILS TO NEGATE ALLEGED ASBESTOS IN ITS BOILERS OR PLAINTIFF'S EXPOSURE THERETO. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: QUENTIN KOPP, REPORTER: WENDY PUN, CSR # 12891 301/QK
